decisions.title: Performance concerns for dashboard project
decisions.detail: There are concerns regarding the performance of the Horizon Dashboard project.

evidence.quote: there are some concerns about performance

decisions.title: Potential need for a better database
decisions.detail: A suggestion was made to consider using a better database for the project at some future point.

evidence.quote: Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.

decisions.title: UI aesthetic improvement required
decisions.detail: The user interface of the dashboard needs to be improved visually to look nicer.

evidence.quote: The UI needs to look nicer.

decisions.title: Current UI design deemed unsatisfactory
decisions.detail: There was a consensus that the current design of the UI is not great.

evidence.quote: We all agreed the current design isn't great.

decisions.title: Project timelines are undecided
decisions.detail: Although timelines were discussed, no firm decisions were made regarding them.

evidence.quote: There was a discussion about timelines but nothing was firmly decided.

decisions.title: Potential future integration with CRM system
decisions.detail: Integration with the CRM system might be required in the future, but the timing and method are currently uncertain.

evidence.quote: We might need to integrate with the CRM system eventually, but we're not sure when or how.

decisions.title: Budget is a concern
decisions.detail: Budget was identified as a concern for the project, although no specific figures were discussed.

evidence.quote: Budget was mentioned briefly — it's a concern but no numbers were shared.

decisions.title: Need for more thorough testing
decisions.detail: The team lead suggested that the testing process should be improved to be more thorough.

evidence.quote: The team lead hinted that testing should be more thorough.

decisions.title: Documentation needs improvement
decisions.detail: It was suggested that documentation practices should be improved for future work.

evidence.quote: We should probably document things better going forward.

implementation_scope: Implement performance optimizations for the Horizon Dashboard system.
Redesign and implement the user interface for the Horizon Dashboard to improve aesthetics and address current design deficiencies.
Enhance existing testing processes and potentially frameworks to ensure more thorough coverage (inferred_needs_confirmation).
Establish and improve documentation practices for future development and update existing documentation (inferred_needs_confirmation).

constraints.text: Performance targets must be met for the Horizon Dashboard project.
Project budget is a concern and acts as a limiting factor, although specific figures are not yet defined.

open_technical_questions: Evaluation is required to determine if the current database system is adequate for performance needs; if not, what alternative "better database" should be adopted (D002 - inferred_needs_confirmation)?
What are the technical requirements, preferred integration method, and target timeline for future integration with the CRM system (D006 - inferred_needs_confirmation)?

acceptance_criteria: Given a defined UI design specification, When the Horizon Dashboard is rendered, Then all visual elements (e.g., colors, typography, spacing, component styles) shall match the approved design specification.

Given a defined UI design specification, When the Horizon Dashboard is viewed across all supported screen resolutions and device types, Then the UI layout and responsiveness shall adhere to the design specification.
test_scenarios: Verify the main dashboard page visually conforms to the new design specifications.
Verify all interactive UI components (buttons, links, forms) reflect the new aesthetic and remain functional across the dashboard.
Verify the aesthetic consistency of the UI across different pages and states of the dashboard (e.g., loading states, error messages).
Execute performance tests to establish baseline load times for key dashboard views under typical user traffic.
Execute stress tests to identify breaking points and performance degradation under peak user loads.
edge_cases: Verify UI aesthetics and responsiveness on the minimum supported screen resolution and aspect ratio.
Verify UI aesthetics and responsiveness on the maximum supported screen resolution and aspect ratio.
unresolved_behaviors: What are the specific performance targets (e.g., maximum load times for key pages, number of concurrent users to support, acceptable response times for interactive elements) that the Horizon Dashboard must meet?
Where can the specific visual design guidelines, mockups, or prototypes for the 'nicer' UI be accessed for verification?

What specific aspects of testing or quality assurance should be made 'more thorough' (e.g., specific test coverage metrics, introduction of new testing methodologies like accessibility testing, security testing, or performance benchmarking targets)?

decision_history: It was agreed by consensus that the current design of the UI is not great (D004).
It was decided that the user interface of the dashboard needs to be improved visually to look nicer (D003).
dependencies: Potential future integration with a CRM system is inferred but currently lacks confirmed timing and method (inferred) (D006).
risks: There are confirmed concerns regarding the performance of the Horizon Dashboard project (D001).
Budget has been identified as a concern for the project, with no specific figures discussed (D007).
alignment_gaps: The specific definition and scope of 'more thorough testing' are not yet clearly defined or confirmed across stakeholders (inferred from D008).
There is no clear agreement or established standard for improved documentation practices for future work (inferred from D009).
open_questions: What are the firm project timelines, key delivery dates, and checkpoints? (PM owned, high impact) (D005)
Is integration with the CRM system part of the current project scope? If so, what are the requirements, estimated timing, and assigned ownership? (PM owned, high impact) (D006)
What are the specific requirements and acceptance criteria for 'more thorough testing' to ensure quality expectations are aligned and met? (PM/QA owned, medium impact) (D008)

Confirm if the 'potential need for a better database' is an immediate requirement to mitigate current performance concerns, or a future technical consideration, and who owns its evaluation? (PM/Eng lead owned, medium impact) (D002)
What improved documentation standards and practices should be established and implemented for future project work? (PM/Dev Lead owned, low impact) (D009)

system_requirements: The Horizon Dashboard UI SHALL be visually improved to meet aesthetic standards (D003).
The Horizon Dashboard system SHALL meet specified performance targets (inferred from D001).
The system testing process SHALL be improved to ensure more thorough coverage (inferred from D008 - needs confirmation).
data_flows: Future data flow between the Horizon Dashboard and an external CRM system is anticipated, with specific data elements and direction to be defined (inferred from D006).
interface_contracts: API contracts for future integration between the Horizon Dashboard and the CRM system require formal definition (inferred from D006).

constraints.text: The project implementation SHALL adhere to an unspecified budget.
The Horizon Dashboard system performance SHALL meet business expectations.
Future system and development documentation SHALL adhere to improved standards (inferred - needs confirmation).

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific non-functional performance requirements (e.g., response times, throughput, concurrency limits) for the Horizon Dashboard system?
What are the current data storage performance and scalability bottlenecks, and what criteria will be used to evaluate alternative database technologies for future implementation (inferred from D002)?

What specific data entities, attributes, and operations are required for the future integration between the Horizon Dashboard and the CRM system, including data synchronization patterns and frequency (inferred from D006)?

What specific system-level documentation standards and deliverables (e.g., data models, API specifications, architectural diagrams) are required to fulfill the improved documentation mandate (inferred from D009)?

stakeholder.business_summary: Our discussion centered on the Horizon Dashboard project, identifying key areas for improvement. There are concerns regarding its current performance and a clear consensus that the user interface needs significant visual and functional enhancements. We also noted that budget is a consideration for moving forward.
key_impact: Improving the user interface will directly enhance user satisfaction and the overall user experience.
Addressing performance issues is critical to ensure the dashboard remains fast and reliable for users, preventing frustration and potential operational slowdowns.
Uncertainty around project timelines and budget could impact the speed and scope of these necessary improvements.
Future integration with our customer relationship management (CRM) system could streamline data access and business processes.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirm if we should plan to upgrade our database technology for improved future performance.
Confirm the necessity and timing of integrating with our customer relationship management (CRM) system.
Confirm the need to improve our product testing process to ensure higher quality releases.
Confirm the importance of improving documentation practices for future projects and maintenance.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the definitive timelines for implementing the necessary dashboard improvements?
What is the allocated budget for addressing performance issues and redesigning the user interface?
What specific measures will be taken to resolve the identified performance concerns for the dashboard?

issues.description: The system requirement 'The Horizon Dashboard system SHALL meet specified performance targets (inferred from D001).' incorrectly states that D001 is 'inferred'. Decision D001 ('Performance concerns for dashboard project') is a 'confirmed' decision, meaning the existence of performance concerns and thus the need to address them is confirmed, even if specific targets are yet to be defined.
issues.suggestion: Remove '(inferred from D001)' and directly reference D001. Rephrase to 'The Horizon Dashboard system SHALL address confirmed performance concerns (D001) and meet specified performance targets.'

issues.description: The business summary mentions 'significant visual and functional enhancements' for the UI. However, decisions D003 ('UI aesthetic improvement required') and D004 ('Current UI design deemed unsatisfactory') primarily emphasize *visual* improvements, not explicitly 'functional enhancements'. While visual improvements can contribute to better user experience/functionality, this slightly broadens the scope beyond the explicit notes.
issues.suggestion: Consider rephrasing to 'significant visual enhancements' or clarifying that these visual improvements are expected to enhance the overall user experience and usability.

review.reviewer_notes: A critical issue was identified in the SA View where a confirmed decision (D001) was incorrectly labeled as 'inferred' in a system requirement, misrepresenting the certainty of the underlying decision. A minor overstatement in the Stakeholder View regarding 'functional enhancements' was also noted. The other role outputs (Engineer, QA, PM) are accurate and well-aligned with the extracted decisions. The critical issue requires the status to be 'NEEDS_IMPROVEMENT'.

evidence_quotes: there are some concerns about performance
